article_meta.title: Federal immigration officers board an elevator with respondents at immigration court in New York on 17 November 2025

reasoning: The model has several issues: (1) It flags quoted text in items 4 and 5 - 'Trump's corrupted Justice Department' and 'ICE abductions' are both inside quotes from Brad Lander, violating the attribution rule. (2) Item 3 about Slant references quoted text as evidence, though the underlying structural observation about source balance is valid author-level bias. (3) Item 1 correctly identifies 'spied' as author word choice. (4) Item 2 correctly identifies an author prose assertion. (5) Item 6 correctly identifies missing attribution in author prose. However, items 4 and 5 are clear attribution rule violations - flagging language inside quotation marks as bias.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: Items 4 and 5 flag quoted text from Brad Lander ('Trump's corrupted Justice Department' and 'ICE abductions'). While the model frames these as the article insufficiently distancing from the quotes, the flagged text itself is inside quotation marks. Item 3 also references quoted text as evidence of slant, though the structural observation is valid. These are clear attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Items 1, 2, and 6 are defensible author-level bias findings. Item 3 (Slant) is a legitimate structural observation though improperly evidenced with quotes. Items 4 and 5 are false positives because they flag quoted source language. So roughly 2 of 6 items are clearly problematic, with item 3 being debatable.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught several important instances. It could have also flagged: 'deportation traps' (used with attribution to AP but the author chose to include this loaded framing), the author's characterization comparing FBI to J. Edgar Hoover era through structural placement, and 'A federal officer was filmed pushing a woman to the floor' as selective detail inclusion. Some misses but not egregious.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Item 1 (Word Choice for 'spied') is correct. Item 2 (Opinion Statements Presented as Fact) is defensible. Item 3 (Slant) is correctly labeled for the structural observation. Item 6 (Omission of Source Attribution) is correctly labeled. Items 4-5 shouldn't have been flagged at all, but their labels are at least in the right category for the text if it were author prose.
